commands.review_summary:
The PR wraps `file_fetch` text result messages with `wrapExternalContent` and adds a mocked regression test for spoofed external-content markers and newline-bearing fetched-file metadata.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +81. Total +90 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main builds `file_fetch` text results directly from node-returned metadata and decoded file text without an external-content wrapper. I did not run a live paired-node Gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is appropriate; the next step is contributor real-behavior proof or an explicit maintainer proof override for this protected PR.

target.title: fix(filefetch): wrap fetched text as external content
target.body:
## Summary

Harden the `file_fetch` text result path by wrapping fetched-file text output with the existing external-content boundary before it reaches the model.

Refs NVIDIA-dev/openclaw-tracking#682.

## Changes

- Wrap `file_fetch` text content with `wrapExternalContent`, including node-returned path/MIME metadata and inline small text file contents.
- Preserve image, media-store, file-transfer policy, node invocation, and saved media behavior.
- Add a focused regression test for spoofed external-content markers and newline-bearing metadata in a small markdown fetch result.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/file-transfer/src/tools/file-fetch-tool.test.ts`
- `node scripts/check-changed.mjs --base upstream/main`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/file-transfer/src/tools/file-fetch-tool.ts extensions/file-transfer/src/tools/file-fetch-tool.test.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Behavior addressed: `file_fetch` text results now carry external-content warning/markers around the returned text result, including fetched-file metadata and inline text contents.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on branch `682`, rebased onto `upstream/main` at `126a3363a3`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the targeted file-transfer Vitest file and the scoped changed check commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: The regression test asserts the tool result contains external-content start/end markers, sanitizes a spoofed end marker, and places newline-bearing fetched-file metadata after the wrapper start marker.

Observed result after fix: Targeted test, scoped changed check, formatting check, diff check, and final autoreview all completed successfully.

What was not tested: A live paired-node `file_fetch` call through a running Gateway was not exercised. Remote Testbox proof was attempted, but this environment has no usable `crabbox` or `blacksmith` binary, so the wrapper failed before allocation.

## Notes

- This is a hardening/parity fix under the current OpenClaw security model; it does not change file-transfer authorization policy or exec/tool approval behavior.
- `CHANGELOG.md` was not changed.
- AI-assisted change. No agent transcript is included because this work references private security triage context.
target.author: eleqtrizit
